findings.summary: LMV358 dual op-amp (U2) not detected as amplifier; RC filter R1/C2: input_net and output_net are swapped relative to signal flow; LDO regulator NCP1117DT33G (U3) correctly detected with +5V input a...

correct.description: U3 is correctly identified as an LDO with topology='LDO', input_rail='+5V', output_rail='VCC'. Decoupling analysis correctly identifies bypass caps on both rails.

incorrect.description: RC filter entry for R1 (12K) + C2 (4.7uF) shows input_net='__unnamed_3' (the R1.2/C2.2 junction, i.e. the mid-node) and output_net='GND'. The true signal input is __unnamed_4 (C2.1 side, connected to U2 non-inverting input and R5), and the output is __unnamed_3 (junction). The type='high-pass' and cutoff of 2.82 Hz are mathematically correct but the net direction labeling is backwards.

RC filters include two 'RC-network' entries that both involve C8 (4.7uF): one pairs R6(1M)+C8 and another pairs R7(8.2K)+C8. C8 is a coupling capacitor between the two op-amp stages; creating multiple RC networks through it with different resistors at each end produces artifacts. The ground_nets are signal nodes (__unnamed_9, __unnamed_6), not GND, indicating these are not true shunt-to-ground filters.

missed.description: U2 (LMV358, dual op-amp) is classified as generic 'ic'. The signal_analysis.amplifiers list is empty. HB100 is a Doppler sensor preamp circuit with two op-amp gain stages built around U2 units 1 and 2. The analyzer should detect the inverting/non-inverting topology of each LMV358 unit.

findings.summary: U2 MIC5504-3.3YMTR LDO has zero pins resolved — topology=unknown, no I/O rails

incorrect.description: In bluetooth.sch, U2 (MIC5504-3.3YMTR, lib_id=LDO_REGULATOR) has an empty pins list. The KiCad5 legacy 'LDO_REGULATOR' symbol has no pin entries in the cache lib, so the analyzer cannot determine IN/OUT connections. Result: topology='unknown', input_rail=null, output_rail=null. The correct detection should show input=+5V, output=+3.3V.

findings.summary: single_pin_nets falsely reports U1 ATmega32U4 GND pin connected to net PC7; Crystal X1 (16MHz) with 22pF load caps correctly detected; USB D+/D- lines present but usb_circuits list is empty; LC fil...

correct.description: Crystal circuit detection correctly identifies X1=16MHz with C5 and C6 (both 22pF), effective load of 14pF (series combination), and marks it within typical range. This is correct for the ATmega32U4 application.
L1/C15 LC filter on the PSEL net is correctly identified. Resonant frequency 7.34 kHz and impedance 2.17 ohm are mathematically correct for these values.

incorrect.description: design_observations lists U1 pin 'GND' on net 'PC7' and pin 'UGnd' on net 'PD0'. These are hardware USB pins of the ATmega32U4 (D+/D-/UGnd); the net names reflect pin mapping from the KiCad5 legacy library where pin names don't match net labels. This is likely a pin-name vs net-name ambiguity in KiCad5 legacy parsing, producing false single-pin-net warnings for what are actually multi-pin nets.

missed.description: The design has J1 USB connector with D+ and D- signals (via R2/R3 series resistors to ATmega32U4 U1). design_observations correctly notes usb_data for D+ and D- but signal_analysis.usb_circuits is empty. The analyzer detected the USB nets but did not produce a USB circuit entry.
